name: Fillout Intake Form + Webhook Pipeline

fillout_form_spec.notes: 8 fields documented with types, validation, and Fillout-specific config. Dropdown options sourced from career-stages.md. Webhook payload shape documented with field extraction strategy.

webhook_handler.notes: Bun HTTP server on configurable port (default 8421). Receives Fillout webhook POST at /intake, extracts fields by name pattern matching, normalizes phone to E.164, downloads+extracts resume text (PDF via pdftotext, DOCX via pandoc), generates caller brief via /zo/ask, stores in DuckDB with upsert on phone, sends SMS notification to V. Health check at /health.

duckdb_schema.notes: caller_profiles table already existed from D3.3 parallel work. D3.2 ensured UNIQUE index on phone column (was non-unique). ensureTable() in intake-webhook.ts is idempotent with CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS + CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS.

phone_lookup_function.notes: Exports: lookupCaller(phone) → CallerProfile|null, updateCallerLastCall(phone), normalizePhone(raw) → E.164 string, CallerProfile interface, generateUUID(). Uses libphonenumber-js for robust phone parsing. D3.1 webhook server should import from this module.

tests_run.phone_normalization: 6/6 passed — US formats (+1, parenthesized, dotted, 10-digit, 11-digit), empty string, international
tests_run.lookup_null_for_missing: Returns null for non-existent phone — PASS
tests_run.insert_and_lookup: Insert profile then lookup by phone — PASS
tests_run.e2e_fillout_webhook: Full Fillout payload → extracted fields → normalized phone (+16175559876) → career stage resolved (outreach) → brief generated → stored in DuckDB → verified via query — PASS
tests_run.server_startup: Starts cleanly, logs port and DB path — PASS

dependencies_for_downstream.D3.1: Import { lookupCaller, updateCallerLastCall, CallerProfile } from './caller-lookup'. On assistant-request, call lookupCaller(callerPhone) and inject caller_brief into system prompt if matched.
dependencies_for_downstream.D4.1: Integration test should verify: (1) Fillout webhook stores profile, (2) lookupCaller returns it, (3) main webhook uses it for personalized greeting.

learnings: DuckDB ON CONFLICT requires UNIQUE index on conflict column. D3.3 brief specifies CREATE INDEX (non-unique) — fixed to CREATE UNIQUE INDEX. Logged to build ledger.
Fillout webhook payload uses 'questions' array with name/type/value — extract by name pattern rather than field ID since IDs are opaque.
known_limitations: Resume extraction depends on pdftotext (for PDF) and pandoc (for DOCX) being installed — both are pre-installed on Zo.
Caller brief generation requires ZO_CLIENT_IDENTITY_TOKEN — falls back to template brief if unavailable.
Intake webhook runs as separate server (port 8421) — D3.1 may choose to integrate it as a route on the main webhook server instead.
